    "title": "Director, Product Marketing (Video)",
    "skills": "Marketing and Sales",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "Cloudinary is looking for a Product Marketing Director to join our team to drive the success of our API-first Video product line. This individual will lead key product efforts and is expected to bring innovative ideas and creative strategies to accelerate Cloudinary’s growth in the industry. You, as the expert, will collaborate with cross-functional teams to generate market awareness and revenue for our Video Platform and Video APIs. Come join us here at Cloudinary.  Responsibilities:  Lead product marketing for the Cloudinary Video product linePosition Cloudinary as a clearly differentiated Video vendor to customers, prospects, analystsBring deep industry insights and subject expertise in the Video space to the product and marketing teamsCraft compelling product messaging and stories for key use cases, industries, personas, and market segments Work with the corporate marketing, demand gen, CSM, and growth teams to drive awareness, campaigns, opportunities, and upsell/cross-sell for Video with prospects and current customersCreate value-based content and sales enablement in the form of presentations, web content, blogs, datasheets, brochures, customer case studies, demos, videos, and training materialsLaunch new innovations to marketAdvise product roadmap in close partnership with product management and product strategy teamsRepresent Cloudinary at thought leadership, demand generation, and company events as a Video SMEAdvise self-serve teams on strategies for positioning, messaging, and packaging/pricing to optimize Video PLG motion Analyze the market and key competitors to articulate differentiatorsWork with the PR and AR teams to brief media and analystsDrive win/loss analysis for Video to understand why we win or lose    About You:  10+ years of product marketing experience (can include product management experience) 3+ years experience in the video space preferred Must have experience with SaaSExcellent writing and presentation skillsExperience with multiple business buyer personas, including technical buyersProven creativity and messaging skillsHighly analytical, data-driven approachComfortable with both high-level strategy and hands-on work                Show more                Show less",
    "employmentType": "Full-time"
}

    "title": "Account Manager",
    "skills": "Sales and Business Development",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "We seek a driven and passionate Account Manager to join our team to drive retention and growth for our enterprise accounts. As a Cloudinary Account Manager, you will be responsible for nurturing and retaining existing client relationships and closing new growth opportunities across Cloudinary’s full suite of products. Prior experience managing accounts and selling Saas in the enterprise space is required. This person must be a self-starter who can manage a large book of customers and create processes at scale.  Responsibilities:  Identify and close expansion opportunities to maximize revenue from existing accountsMonitor client usage and performance metrics to identify areas for improvement and optimizationUnderstand clients' business objectives and challenges to position Cloudinary's solutions effectivelyCollaborate with cross-functional teams, including Sales, Customer Success, and Product, to deliver exceptional service and client supportAct as a trusted advisor to clients, providing guidance on best media management and optimization practicesMaintain accurate and current account and opportunity forecasting within internal sales toolsStay updated on industry trends, competitive landscape, and emerging technologies to serve clients betterEnsure customer satisfaction and retention    About You:  3+ years of experience in an account management role Proven history of over-achievement and meeting targets in account management and/or sales roles within the SaaS industryStrong understanding of cloud-based technologies and servicesExcellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively present technical information to both technical and non-technical audiencesAbility to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment    Why Join Cloudinary:  Opportunity to work with cutting-edge technology and industry-leading clientsA collaborative and inclusive work culture that values creativity, innovation, and diversityCompetitive compensation package with performance-based incentivesFlexible remote work options and opportunities for career advancement and professional development    If you are passionate about helping clients succeed in the digital world and thrive in a fast-paced, innovative environment, we want to hear from you! Apply now to join our team as a Cloudinary Account Manager.              Show more                Show less",
    "employmentType": "Full-time"
}

    "description": "We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Leader of Global Commercial Account Management/Growth to join our team with a focus on managing and developing a team of Account Managers to solve key challenges for clients while achieving retention and revenue growth. Responsibilities:  Lead and Manage a team of Commercial Account Managers focused on retention and growth revenueManage, Hire, and Lead performance conversations with team membersNavigate client relationships across a technical portfolio of productsAutomate certain touch points to effectively manage the team's large book of business Accurately forecast growth and retention opportunitiesEscalate customer questions and issues to the appropriate teams    About You:  Minimum of 3+ years of experience in a customer-facing, B2B, revenue-generating leadership role in Saas/technology space 5+ years of experience in software account management and/or sales requiredStrong organization, presentation, and influencing skillsHistory of exceeding sales and retention goals Highly motivated as well as able to develop a highly motivated and success-oriented teamProven ability to integrate knowledge across disciplines to include relationship management, consultative selling, closing contracts, operation/process flow, and product function                Show more                Show less",

{
    "title": "Trainer and Instructional Designer (Consultant)",
    "skills": "Education and Training",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "We seek an energetic Trainer and Instructional Designer with strong communication skills and expertise in training delivery and curriculum development. As a consultant for 6-9 months, you will play a key role in delivering impactful virtual and on-site training for Cloudinary’s customers, empowering them to optimize their image and video management efforts. This role is pivotal in fulfilling our customer obligations, supporting growth in existing accounts, and enhancing the adoption of Cloudinary products.  You will also leverage your instructional design skills to shape and adapt engaging, effective training content tailored to our customers’ needs.  Why Join Us:  This position offers a unique opportunity to play a key role in our growth and expansion efforts. By working with our team, you will contribute to the success of our customers and the overall business, ensuring that our training programs are effective, scalable, and impactful.  If you are passionate about training delivery, instructional design, technology, and customer success, we invite you to apply and help us achieve our business goals through exceptional education experiences.  Responsibilities:  Instructional Design and Curriculum Development: Design and develop engaging, user-focused training content, including ILT and vILT, applying adult learning principles to align with customer use cases and Cloudinary product offeringsTraining Delivery: Deliver tailored training sessions, both on-site and virtually, to diverse global audiences, ensuring mastery of Cloudinary products like Programmable Media and Cloudinary AssetsSubject Matter Expertise: Develop deep expertise in Cloudinary’s products to address customer questions confidently and refine training content based on the latest product updates and use casesCustomer Collaboration: Partner with Customer Success and cross-functional teams to integrate customer challenges and goals into impactful training solutionsPerformance and Impact Assessment: Measure training program success through product adoption and usage metrics, providing actionable feedback to improve curriculum and deliveryProject Management: Oversee multiple training projects and their associated timelines, resources, and deliverables, ensuring seamless integration with other Customer Success initiatives for the customers    Measures of Success  Training Delivery: Number of sessions delivered, customer satisfaction scores, and engagement metrics like attendance and participationLearning Outcomes: Increased product adoption, usage rates, and demonstrable customer proficiencyInstructional Design: High-quality, relevant training materials with positive stakeholder and participant feedbackCustomer Business Impact: Improved retention and expansion rates for trained accounts and positive feedback from Customer Success teamsOperational Efficiency: Timely delivery of projects and effective adaptation of training with minimal revisions    About You:  Proven experience in technical training and instructional design, particularly for software products, with delivery to users of varying technical expertiseExceptional communication, presentation, and facilitation skills, capable of distilling complex technical concepts into accessible, engaging instructionStrong understanding of adult learning methodologies, instructional design frameworks (e.g., ADDIE, SAM), and e-learning platformsCollaborative and adaptable, excelling in a fast-paced, cross-functional environmentWillingness to travel up to 50% for on-site training sessions, with the ability to tailor content to global audiencesExperience with Cloudinary is a significant advantageEastern Time Zone preferred to support North America and EMEA customers    Additional Details:  While most training sessions will be delivered virtually, travel to customer locations may be required for on-site training, not exceeding 50%As a consultant, weekly hours for this role may vary based on project needs, with some weeks requiring up to 40 hours of work and others with no assigned hours. Flexibility in scheduling will be necessary to accommodate business priorities and deliverables              Show more                Show less",
    "employmentType": "Full-time"
}

{
    "title": "Director, Partner Program",
    "skills": "Business Development and Sales",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "Cloudinary was founded in 2012 to solve a simple problem: making it an easy platform to manage images & videos. Over the years we evolved into a $120 million+ annual recurring revenue business serving 10,000 companies across industries – from the smallest Shopify stores to iconic global brands and other worldwide companies.  Cloudinary is now the foundational technology for all images and videos on the internet. Only we provide robust, flexible, and intelligent solutions for every business in the world to harness the power of visual media to tell its story. As a single source of truth for brands’ image and video needs, we also provide a comprehensive array of APIs and integrations to transform every brand into a masterful visual storyteller.  If images and video are the electricity powering the new digital world, Cloudinary is the power grid: enabling anyone to easily plug in and “light up” their business with high-impact visual media, without worrying about the science behind the electrical current.  Cloudinary is passionate about building valuable partnerships that create joint wins for us, our partners, prospects, and our shared customers. To continue to drive the growth of our ecosystem, we’re looking for a Director, Channel Programs, who will set the direction and roadmap for the overall Partner Program. This role will define and lead key initiatives and program changes to increase the program value to partners and Cloudinary.  The Director, Channel Programs helps manage and support the growth of the channel business. They are responsible for the day to day channel program development/operation as well as the execution of our partner program. The Director, Channel Programs reports into the VP, GTM Operations and Enablement; and works cross-functionally with channel & alliances, revenue enablement, marketing, sales, and product to integrate channel programs throughout the business.  Responsibilities:  Responsible for the design, management & execution of Cloudinary’s global partner programManage Cloudinary’s Partner Enablement Certification & Badge Model. Work closely with Revenue Enablement and Partner Marketing on partner enablement tools/training and implement as part of the overall partner programProvide management of Partner Portal to create an engaging platform for partners to interact with Cloudinary; including deal registration, Sales engagement, opportunity tracking, commissions, and partner success programs as well as supporting partner eventsWork with channel operations to implement channel strategy, data/reporting, system workflows, and overall operational governance for the programWork cross-functionally with marketing, product, legal and finance to continue to implement and grow partner programs and ensure alignment across the businessCollaborate across C&A to gather requirements for Programs, working closely with regional C&A team on program executionDesign, build and manage Ecosystem Programs including incentive programs, MDF Program, Reseller, Services, Alliances, Deal Registration Program, etcDesign program guides that detail requirements and benefits, terms and conditions, etcManage communications to partners via portal, newsletters, webcasts, social channels. Support the development of messaging and content for partner eventsDocument policies and processes to support all aspects of the program, including field / channel rules of engagement and enable global executionDesign & launch field enablement on Ecosystem Program, Rules of Engagement, Deal Reg, How to Partner, etc. Collaborate with Revenue Enablement in the development of enablement materialsCollect and manage feedback from partners into the ongoing roadmap for the Ecosystem ProgramEstablish and track metrics of the program, run a quarterly compliance process with the Channel & Alliances fieldWork with the Revenue enablement and Customer education teams to update and manage Partner education and enablement materials and experienceDevelop Partner Program collateral/documents and onboarding tools to enhance overall partner experience and communicate to partnersCommunicate channel programs, strategies & events and updates internally to drive alignment throughout the business    About you:  Bachelor’s degree with 8+ years demonstrated progressive experience in building and managing partner programsExperience building and managing Ecosystem programs. Programs should include incentive, deal registration, MDF, emerging markets and other programs to drive value for partners and CloudinaryExperience building channel services & practices in SaaS/subscription modelsExcellent communication, analytical and organizational skillsExperience working with partner accreditation programs, specifically with regards to compliance tracking and enforcementExecution & revenue focusedGreat project & program management skills, ability to leverage, manage & lead virtual teamsEast Coast preferred for global timezones    Cloudinary is proud to be an equal opportunity employer dedicated to pursuing a diverse workforce.              Show more                Show less",
    "employmentType": "Full-time"
}

    "title": "CTO - Co-founder, AI-driven Moderation",
    "skills": "Information Technology",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "We are here to build Cloudinary’s next big thing. We accelerate Cloudinary’s business growth by quickly developing & validating new product ideas in search of product-market fit. The potential impact is huge, we have a lot on our plate, and we could use some help around here.  Cloudinary is seeking an R&D Lead to lead the development of Moderation, a new product being built in its Emerging Business group. Moderation will leverage visual media AI to perform brand-aligned moderation of images and videos. We’re a small-sized, high-paced, and incredibly independent team. We take full end-to-end ownership of our product - from design and development to deployment and testing.  Responsibilities  Take a hands-on role in all development efforts, operating as both a leader and an individual contributor, like a Co-founder & CTOCo-lead the venture alongside its GM as a counterpartDevelop deep understanding of the target user persona’s needs, and passion for the underlying market problem, to build a product that would delight themLeverage Cloudinary’s powerful platform to get a product to market as soon as possible, only developing what will move the needleConstantly adjust focus to what is needed to make the product/venture successful    Requirements  10+ years of development experience3+ years of development manager experienceProficient in Python and have experience with machine learning/LLM/Gen AI libraries and frameworksExtensive experience serving large Enterprise customers - a strong advantageStrong hands-on experience with cloud platforms, preferably AWSKnowledge of image or video technologies is an advantageKnowledge of cloud-based architectures and web technologiesEnthusiastic, humble, and user-centricEager to learn new things    Cloudinary is a leading cloud-based image and video management platform, and this is an excellent opportunity to join a growing company and be a part of the develop ment and growth of a new, exciting product. The team is initially small and focused on product and R&D, with additional disciplines added as the product gains traction. If you are a highly experienced and motivated individual with a passion for building successful products, we would love to hear from you.              Show more                Show less",

{
    "title": "Lead Developer Experience Engineer",
    "skills": "Information Technology",
    "Industry": "Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage",
    "seniority": "Not Applicable",
    "description": "The web is visual and we’re always looking for new team members who possess the skills, energy, passion, and commitment to join Cloudinary’s mission of empowering developers to build and deliver great image and video experiences. Cloudinary is the image and video platform for the Web, geared first and foremost for developers, and ensuring excellent developer experience is a top priority!  We are always looking for new team members who possess the skills, energy, passion, and commitment to join Cloudinary’s mission of empowering developers to build and deliver great image and video experiences. Cloudinary is the image and video platform for the Web, geared first and foremost for developers, and ensuring excellent developer experience is a top priority!  Cloudinary’s DevX team is looking for an experienced Lead Developer Experience Engineer, a software engineer (preference for specialty in frontend web development) that strives to improve the DX of our image and video platform, with an emphasis on educating and inspiring other developers via creation and delivery of interactive content. KG  You support our team as    Plant Controller / Production Controller (f/m/d)     Responsibilities      As part of a 5-person team, you will contribute to the financial controlling of our production site, preparing and analyzing cost and profitability reports.  You analyze the Profit & Loss (P&L) statement and ensure accurate booking of all business transactions. You support management decisions with well-founded financial analyses and ad-hoc reports.  You create and monitor budgets, forecasts, and investment plans.  You review and manage production KPIs to ensure efficiency and profitability.  You collaborate closely with production and logistics teams to analyze cost structures and material flows.  Furthermore, you continuously develop controlling tools and processes at the site.     Qualifications      You have successfully completed a degree in business administration, controlling, industrial engineering or a comparable field.  You have several years of professional experience in plant or production controlling, ideally in an industrial environment.  You are proficient in SAP (CO/FI modules) and MS Excel.  You have a strong affinity for numbers and experience in dealing with big data.  You communicate clearly and effectively, ask critical questions in complex matters and enjoy working independently as well as within a team.  You are fluent in German and have good English skills.
